      "authenticationType": "none",
      "destination": "ui5"
    },
    {
      "source": "^(.*)$",
      "target": "$1",
      "service": "html5-apps-repo-rt",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa"
    }
  ]
}

 

and below code should be added to the View 1 controller of second UI Module.

 

  var sURL = "restfulapi/objects/4";
            var data = jQuery.ajax({
                method: "GET",
                async: false,
                url: sURL,
                success: function (data) {
                    console.log("in success");
                }
            });

 

Step 8: Build and deploy the project to cloud foundry

Right click on the mta.yaml file and select 'Build MTA Project' to start the project build.

Once the build is successfully completed, a new folder 'mta_archives' will be added to the MTA project as shown below.

The mta_archives folder will contain a .mtar file which will be used for deployment to cloud foundry.

Right click on the .mtar  file and select deploy option.

Note that system may prompt you to enter your Cloud Foundry credentials. Kindly enter the credentials and select correct API Endpoint and Org Space.

Once, application is successfully deployed, You will be able to find those applications in the cockpit under HTML5 Applications tab.

When you click on the link, you should be able to check the service call in network tab as shown below.

 

With this, we come to the end of this blog post. I hope this blog post proves to be a useful resource in the future.

entry.title: Overview of all GenAI Options in SAP Build Code
entry.content:
Introduction 

SAP Build Code, the pro-code offering utilizing Joule,  brings cutting-edge generative AI (GenAI) capabilities to developers across a wide array of use cases. These range from UI5 and Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) development to providing assistance on Mobile Development Kit (MDK) projects or HANA development.

Since its general availability in March 2024, which initially highlighted AI features primarily for CAP, Joule's GenAI capabilities have expanded significantly. As new features continue to be introduced, this blog post aims to summarize the current features and the variety of use cases they support. Furthermore, as the approach for the use of different functionalities may differ, we will also provide some recommendations that are specific to some features.

Before introducing the list we will dive shortly into what prompting is as well as talk about the difference between general LLM use and Joule for develompent. (Co-Author: @MarcHuber )

**Note: This blog will be updated from time to time to reflect upcoming and new features (See end of post)

What are Prompts? 

"Prompt" is a verb that means to cause, help, or instruct someone or something to do something. In information technology, "prompt" can refer to the "command prompt". 

Prompts are used in GenAI, including SAP Joule, across several topics. A prompt can be a command, input, or a piece of text provided to initiate a response or action. SAP Joule can utilize prompts as instructions to propose code. 

An example of a simple prompt is: "Create an incident management app." 

 

Importance of well-crafted Prompts in generating Code 

Generative AI models – including the ones used by SAP Joule – use prompts as a point of initiation, where they are given initial instructions or context to generate content. Artificial intelligence capabilities are especially useful for tasks that don’t need to consider things like cultural differences. So, app development is a great candidate for being supported using prompts. Effective prompt engineering implies creating well-structured, directive prompts that steer AI responses to ensure they are accurate and relevant to the context. 

The code generated by SAP Joule significantly depends on the quality of input prompts. Therefore, the precision and relevance of the output can be greatly improved by constructing high-quality prompts that draw out the intended response. Such carefully engineered prompts can notably boost user experience and task coherence. 

It is important to remember that crafting an effective prompt necessitates creativity. It is your responsibility to ensure your expected output is clearly conveyed to the model. This commonly includes comprehension of how the model perceives different prompts and the appropriate rephrasing of them.

 

Differences between general LLM use and using Joule for coding tasks inside  SAP Business Application Studio 

Prompt engineering is essential when using a general LLM to ensure good response quality. Prompt engineering methods such as Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting have been shown to achieve better results. Chain-of-Thought prompting involves guiding individuals to articulate their reasoning step-by-step, helping them clarify their thoughts and explore the reasoning behind their conclusions. This technique encourages deeper cognitive engagement and can enhance problem-solving and decision-making skills by making the thought process explicit. An example of this could be adding the following to the prompt:

“[prompt question]. Please break down your thought process step-by-step.” 

Joule in SAP Build Code, however, is specialized in supporting pro-code developers in coding tasks. It provides predefined slash commands for a variety of use cases, such as UI5, Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP), Mobile, and HANA development. Due to the specialization of Joule, the prompt will already be matched and improved, thus reducing the manual effort to make use of extensive prompt engineering methods. 

This chapter will further depict best practices for using Joule in SAP Build Code. 

 

Be clear, specific, and directive  

Generally, in prompt engineering it is always good to provide more context while being clear on what you want. The more precise information the prompt contains, the better Joule will understand what you really want to achieve. Example:  

  • Vague Prompt: "I need a purchase order tracking application."  
  • Improved Prompt: "Develop a purchase order tracking application where entity 'Purchase Order' is associated to entity 'Supplier'. The 'Purchase Order' consists of the following fields: ID, Quantity, Price. Entity 'Supplier' consist of the fields  ID, Name, Contact Person, Mail Address"  

It should be noted that, instead of adding more and more complexity and context into one prompt where Joule for example is used to generate multiple files (e.g. for UI5 development), in some cases breaking complex tasks into simpler ones may be a better approach.  

 

Provide Examples 

Additionally, examples provide meaningful information on how the output should be structured or content should be generated. It can be extremely helpful in some scenarios, for example in sample data generation: 

  • Before: "Create meaningful sample data for the 'Purchase Order' and 'Supplier' entities.” 
  • Improved Prompt: "Create a set of meaningful sample data for the 'Purchase Order' and 'Supplier' entities. The purchase order data should be targeted at German customers and must use the euro currency symbol (€) and the German abbreviation for quantity units (e.g., 'STK' for Stück). Each Purchase Order ID should be exactly 6 digits long. Include at least 20 records. Example format: Purchase Order - ID: 000109; Quantity: 10 STK; Price: 45,25€."

 

Use of context-awareness 

Joule in SAP Build Code in general is context aware of the project you created in the SAP Build Lobby. Depending on the project type (Full-Stack, Fiori, Mobile), certain slash commands will be suggested. Besides that, Joule can make use of files in the project. Depending on the slash command and the prompt description, only required files are considered in the prompt, thereby eliminating the need to use more input data (code) than necessary for the response generation.  

Furthermore, you can make use of the #-sign in a prompt to include files which should be taken into account by Joule. This feature use will be explained in more detail in the following sections.

 

GenAI Options in SAP Build Code 

Joule offers specific skills for SAP app development with SAP Build Code which can be requested using slash commands (“/”). As each slash command serves a different purpose, the required input as well as the output format from within Joule can vary. Therefore, there are best practices for some of the tasks, which will be highlighted in this section. 

 

CAP 

Joule offers skills for the generation and development of CAP. The following slash commands are suggested when creating a Full-Stack Application Project:

/cap-gen-app

For the purpose of creating a CAP application, which involves different parts such as cds model, sample data, and application logic, this command along with a prompt stating these requirements can be triggered. Joule will then support in refining the different parts and generate the whole application at the end.

The prompt can either be precise and context-rich or one can also start with a more vague prompt, since Joule will give guidance. An example for the latter could be:

  • Example: “Create an incident management application with Customers, Indicents, ReplacementItems, and Products. Provide some meaningful sample date.When an incident title containts the word 'urgent' then automatically add logic to set the incident priority to high. ” 

The results looks as follows:

In the sample above Joule splits the app creation process up into multiple tasks and generated some suggestions for each prompt. This serves as an entry point to then further adapt each prompt with the edit button, depending on the requirements. The play button then subsequently gets enabled and triggers the generation of the code, which can be viewed as staging files. This has to be done for all steps. If satisfactory, the application code can be accepted and all the artefacts will be created in the project. All done within the intial slash command. It should be noted, that this advanced application generation process is only triggered when providing  additional information besides the model data, such as sample data or application logic. For further information on this new feature, check out this blog post. 

 

/cap-gen-model 

Besides the multi-agent support from the abovementioned /cap-gen-app command, /cap-gen-model can be utilizied for the single purpose of creating a data model. Joule will create a CAP model along with some sample data based on the prompt. It is recommended to be clear and precise while providing a good amount of context. The following example highlights how being more specific and providing more context can result in better results. 

  • Example: “ I would like to create an incident management application. Can you do that for me?” 

This may provide already some good looking results. However, as in most cases requirements already exist for the entities, its associations, and fields, being more specific can provide the desired outcomes.

With a more specific prompt: 

  • Example: “Create an app where customers can report incidents. Include a 'Customer' entity with fields Customer ID, Name, Email, and Phone Number. Each 'Incident' should include Incident ID, Description, DateReported (Date), and Status. Each incident also includes replacement items, and each replacement item is linked to a product. The 'ReplacementItem' entity should have fields Replacement ID, Quantity , and ReplacementDate (Date). The 'Product' entity should have fields Product ID, Product Name, Category, and ExpiryDate (Date). Use associations instead of compositions for the relationships between entities.” 

In this example, the named entities are defined with the properties coming from requirements. Providing comprehensive descriptions of entities and associations ensures that Joule closely adheres to the application's context during creation. Since Joule prioritizes the creation of compositions over associations, it is advisable to explicitly specify the use of associations as illustrated in the example above. 

If the generated data from the data model does not meet expectations, there is the option to either regenerate the entire dataset to obtain a potentially more suitable outcome, or preferably, use the /cap-edit-model command to make adjustments. 

 

/cap-edit-model 

By using this command, you can edit an existing data model: 

As displayed in the screenshot, a context variable must be entered with the #-sign, so that Joule knows which data model to modify.  

But it is more than just editing an existing data model. You can do code reviews, add comments and much more. You can easily ask like “Does this code follow the best pratices of CAP?” 

You can find more examples in this blog .

 

/cap-gen-data and /cap-edit-data 

Sample data from the CAP app generation can be generated to match realistic values. For general sample data, a vaguer prompt can be used: 

  • Example: “Create realistic values for the sample data” 

Of course, with more precise information, the data will be matched better to the desired format: 

  • Example: “Create realistic values for the sample data. For the Product Entity, make sure the `criticality` values are in the range of [1(Negative), 2(Critical), 3(Positive)]. [...]” 

For further editing the created sample data or adapting existing sample data of a specific entity, /cap-edit-data slash command should be used. A context variable referencing the csv file with existing sample data has to be provided. 

To ensure the generated data meets expectations, it is crucial to use clear and precise prompts. For instance, indicating specific ranges, formats, or patterns can help tailor the output to match the desired criteria. Do not include any detail about the application logic in the data model as it will be ignored by Joule. 

 

/cap-app-logic 

A further pivotal command in the CAP suite is the /cap-app-logic command, which enables the creation and modification of application logic. This command requires a context variable and an application logic file, serving as prerequisites. For more intricate application logic, breaking down the task into smaller, manageable modifications is advisable. 

 

/cap-unit-test 

To create unit tests, this command should be executed after the application logic has been implemented. Considering the context variable of the application logic file, the provided sample prompt for generating a unit test may already be adequate. If additional test scenarios or modifications are necessary, the slash command can be invoked again to make the required adjustments. For unit tests, provide the appropriate data for positive and negative cases to make sure the tests pass accordingly. 

 

Fiori/UI5 

This section describes the use of slash commands for Fiori development, which can be UI5 freestyle apps, Fiori Elements apps, or a mixture between these two using the flexible programming model. 

/fiori-gen-spec-app 

In addition to /cap-gen-app, the /fiori-gen-spec-app slash command facilitates the rapid creation of SAP Fiori applications by translating business requirements into a CAP project with a SAP Fiori elements application. This command leverages the GenAI capabilities to interpret text or image-based inputs, defining entities, associations, and UI layouts based on your specifications. It's designed to aid developers by generating list reports, object pages, and form entry object pages, all while supporting draft-enabled applications by default. 

Since this creates a whole application, it is suggested to provide enough and precise context of the business requirements to the prompt to ensure accurate generation and thus avoid regenerating the whole project. This is also important as regenerating the project will create a completely new project without being able to take over specific parts. Here, Joule also supports an image/sketch as an input.

  • Example (Text):

 

“ I want to create an SAP Fiori application that shows my customers with their contracts. Each customer has the following attributes: 
1. Name 
2. Identification Number 
3. Address 
4. Contract Information 
5. A contract is referencing a customer. One customer can have multiple 
  contracts. Attributes of a contract include: 
6. Contract ID: A unique identifier for the contract 
7. Customer: Information about the customer involved 
8. Contract Type: The type of contract (sales, service, employment) 
9. Start Date: The date on which the contract becomes valid 
10. End Date: The date on which the contract expires (if applicable) 
11. Status: The current status of the contract (draft, active, expired, terminated, etc.) 

When starting the CAP application I first want to see a list of customers. 
When selecting a specific row in that list, I want to see to a page with details of that customer. 

It should show the following sections: 
- section "Customer Info" displaying the attributes of the selected customer 
- section "Contracts" showing a list of all contracts for this customer 

When selecting a specific row in the contracts list, I want to see a page with details of that contract. This page should show two sections: 

- section "Contract Info" containing: 
 - contract ID 
 - Customer Name 
 - Contract Type 
 - Status 
- section "Validity" containing 
 - Start Date 
 - End Date “

 

  • Example (Image):  

For more information, you can refer to the documentation of this slash command. 

 

/ui5 

The /ui5 slash command offers a broad support for many of the topics resolving around UI5 development. For example, it can be used to create and modify files in a freestyle project. This can range from adding controls and application logic to helping with configuration of the project. Furthermore, if asked, Joule can help understand UI5 related artifacts. Besides freestyle development, it also provides help understand Fiori elements and other UI5 related applications. Being clear and precise is recommended for using this command when generating code. It will try to automatically figure out which files in the project are needed as context and include these. At this stage, however, it does not know anything about e.g. the metadata of a remote service. 

  • Example: “Create a table for the main view based on the entity set ‘Products’ of the OData service, which is represented in the local metadata file. Add the following three columns and bindings to the table: ProductID, Name, Quantity.” 
  • Example: “Add a column to the table in the main view which displays a button called ‘Order’. If the user presses the button, show a message toast with the message ‘Order clicked’” 
  • Example: “What does the code in the main controller do?” 
  • Example: “How can I configure an OData service to be used in the app?” 

Furthermore, the prompt can also consider the currently selected code. For example, if a function inside a controller file is selected, the user can simply ask Joule to explain that code without the need to specify the file in the prompt. 

You can refer to this blog on how to get started with UI5 freestyle development using Joule.

/ui5-typescript 

The SAPUI5 TypeScript assistant can be used for converting a SAPUI5 application project (or single files in it) to TypeScript. It can do both, the creation of the appropriate project setup that handles transpilation, as well as converting JavaScript files to TypeScript, which includes the required structural changes in the files, like using ECMAScript (ES) module imports and ES classes. 

The converted or added files will be listed and can be clicked to inspect a diff view of the changes before accepting them. The diff may be extensive, as such a conversion typically touches many lines. Still, this offers an opportunity to identify any unintended changes. 

A certain amount of re-work of the result may be needed, e.g. to adjust some of the generated return types of functions or to add needed typecasts. 

The functionality itself tries to convert everything inside a project at once. However, in order ut to avoid being hit by size limits and inaccuracies, it is recommended to proceed in smaller steps. For example, first convert only the project setup, but no JavaScript files. Then migrate single or few files at once, depending on their size. It is also recommended to only use this assistant for SAPUI5 application code. 

This assistant is generally available inside SAPUI5 projects. 

  • Example: “Migrate the setup of the project to TypeScript. Do not migrate JS files yet.” 
  • Example: “Migrate the Main controller to TypeScript” 
  • Example: “Migrate the current file to TypeScript” 

Further information can be found in this blog post .

 

/ui5-create-page 

The SAPUI5 Create Page assistant creates a new UI5 view with a corresponding controller and configures routing for seamless navigation to this new page. 

This command scaffolds a new XML view and JavaScript or TypeScript controller, updates the manifest.json to include the necessary routing configurations, and provides default code for controller functionality. It can also add code to existing controllers which trigger the navigation to the new page. It is ideal for quickly setting up additional screens or views in your UI5 project. 

  • Example: “Create a new page with controller and routing setup. The new page should contain an address form, navigation should be triggered by a new button in the header of the Main view.” 

 

Mobile 

For mobile app development using mobile services, Joule also offers slash commands. The prerequisite for using these slash commands is having a mobile app project. 

/mdk-gen-i18n 

This command generates i18n translation files in multiple languages, each containing key-value pairs of strings. Based on your business needs, you can create additional translation files for various languages from a source file (e.g. i18n.properties). This enables you to reach a wide audience and ensure that your application is accessible to users in diverse regions. It is recommended to provide the required languages in the prompt itself: 

  • Example: “Please generate i18n files for the following languages: German, Dutch, Italian, and French.” 
  • Example: “Please generate i18n file in German language.” 

Once accepted, you must choose whether to overwrite or merge the changes. Merge file option append any new changes to existing files. Overwrite file option completely replaces an existing file with the new changes. 

 

/mdk-gen-page  

This command generates a new page containing MDK UI controls. Also, it may also produce other related application definitions, such as Action, other associated Page, Rule or Style files, with the page. The service definition is considered when creating the page. Nonetheless, it is possible to specify the desired layout appearance: 

  • Example: “Please generate a customer list page using object table section where firstname and lastname are displayed as headline. The other labels should be empty. 
  • Example: “Please generate a page displaying customer information in a contact cell control.” 

 

/mdk-gen-action 

This command generates a new MDK action. Also, it may also produce other related application definitions, such as Page or other associated Action files, with the Action. The service definition is considered when creating the Action. 

  • Example: “Please generate an action displaying product information.” 
  • Example: “Please generate an action updating product entity.” 
  • Example: “Please generate an action to call SAP AI chat completions API.” 

Note: All the MDK related slash commands are also accessible for a mobile project created in a full-stack project. 

 

HANA

/hana-gen 

This command enables users to create SAP HANA Cloud projects by specifying requirements in natural language. The tool interprets these requirements and generates a complete project structure, including database artifacts such as tables, views, and sample data. This functionality is particularly useful for rapid prototyping and development, ensuring consistency and reducing manual effort. Example prompts: 

  • Example: “Create an inventory management project and use schema name as 'inventory'.” 
  • Example: “Create a flight management project.” 
  • Example: “Create a bookshop management project and use namespace as 'com.sap' and use schema name as 'bookshop'” 

 

/hana-modeling-help

Joule offers also support on modeling questions, such as calculation views and modeling tasks. This feature can either be triggered trough the slash command or via the BAS command palette : "SAP HANA Modeling: Modeling Help (Joule)".

Here are some example prompts:

  • Example: “How to model a calculation view in BAS?” 
  • Example: "How do I add a data source to a calculation view?"
  • Example: “How do I create an hdbtable in BAS? Please give an example.'"

 

SQL generation using natural language

For the SAP HANA Database Explorer extension in SAP Build Code it is possible to create SQL statements from natural language. The prompt has to be provided in the SQL Concsole and then the execution can be triggered via the "Run Prompt" option. During the SQL statement generation the prompt along with additional information about SAP HANA is utilizied to generate syntetically valid SQL statements. However, the output has of course to be checked and validated by the user.

 

Other Slash Commands 

/help 

The /help command displays a list of prompts available in Joule for SAP Build Code. This includes example prompts and descriptions of each prompt. 

/sap-help

Questions regarding solutions,frameworks, and other information from the SAP Help Portal can be asked from within this command. So, this powerful feature provides access to a huge knowledge source.

/code-search 

Natural language can be utilized to search for code and obtain a concise summary of its functionality. The first time a search is performed, it may take some moments for the results to appear because the workspace content needs to be indexed. The results show the files relevant to the search criteria. 

The next time a code search is performed, Joule suggests prompts based on the previous search. Any new file that has been added to the workspace is indexed for code search. 

 

Inline Code-Completion  

Inline code-completion is a feature that provides code snippet suggestions directly in the editor itself (e.g. for JavaScript) based on your typing input the the context. It will be enabled per default. To use inline code-completion effectively, you can accept a full suggestion by pressing Tab or reject it with Esc. For more control, cmd/ctrl + right arrow (→) can be used to accept suggestions word-by-word. The autocomplete suggestion considers the code around the cursor and recent files to provide a matching suggestion. Furthermore, it may be helpful to write a comment with the desired output to get an autocomplete suggestion: 

  • Example: “// Create a Message Box Popup which has three actions: Save, Draft, and Cancel” 

In the example above, after pressing enter, an autocomplete suggestion will be provided based on the comment.  

 

Final Remarks 

While utilizing GenAI functionality can enhance development efficiency, it is recommended to thoroughly review the proposed code before implementation. Although Joule strives to deliver optimal results, Language Learning Models (LLMs) are not perfect. Additionally, response generation may differ even when using identical prompts. Therefore, if the outcome is unsatisfactory, you may either regenerate the response with the same prompt or revise the prompt to achieve better results.  

For example, if a data schema has been created that mostly meets the requirements but is missing a property or definition, it may be an option to rewrite the prompt by including the provided structure from the last prompt and specifically describing what parts are missing.  

As Joule in SAP Build Code is tailored to specific use cases, a don’t is to ask questions unrelated to the slash commands. An example would be, to ask for the weather, request SAP unspecific text generation, etc. The core strength of Joule within SAP Build Code and the Business Application Studio lies in its powerful tailored capabilities, where a clear and precise prompt will help you with what you want to achieve. 

 

***History - Added features
FeatureAdded
/hana-modeling-help01/2025
SQL generation using natural language01/2025
/cap-gen-model01/2025
/cap-gen-app (Advanced app generation)01/2025
/sap-help01/2025

entry.title: SAP UI5 Frontend Application - Debug using AI Assistance in Chrome DevTools
entry.content:
Enhancing Coding Experience with AI Assistance in Chrome DevTools

AI assistance in Chrome DevTools has multiple benefits that can significantly enhance your coding experience. 

Firstly, it provides intelligent code completion and suggestions, helping to reduce errors and increase productivity. With AI analyzing patterns in your code, it offers relevant suggestions and auto-complete code snippets to save you time and effort.

Additionally, AI-powered dev tools enable advanced debugging capabilities. By using machine learning algorithms, it identifies and highlights potential bugs, making it easier to locate and resolve issues in your code. This real-time error detection can save hours of solving time-consuming debugging.

Furthermore, AI-powered dev tools can assist in code updating, offering automated suggestions for optimizing and rearranging code to improve performance and readability. 

You just have to right-click on the element and click on “Ask AI”. 

Explanation starts providing on how to solve the error

Sources Tab

You just have to right-click on the element and click on “Ask AI”. 

Console Errors:

  • You can understand and solve the errors in your code. Just right-click on the error line and select understand this warning. 

AI Provides insights on how to fix the code with proper SAP UI5 Best Practices

Network Tab - Ask AI

Interact with Chat based conversation to find the network traces or traffic

Final Words

AI assistance in Chrome DevTools is a game-changer for web developers. By providing real-time insights, enhancing styling and layout understanding, optimizing performance, simplifying network request analysis, and enabling safe experimentation, it can significantly enhance your coding experience. Embrace this innovative tool and take your web development skills to the next level.

entry.title: Get the menu to the top of Business Application Studio
entry.content:
So a new Development Space has a default that may not be in your personal favour. Here a way to change things.

Go to "Settings" and search for Classic

Change entry from compact to classic

Select visible

and you end with this now:

Hope you find your prefered settings. Somehow this gets overwritten from time to time. This is fast way to change things.

BR Volker

entry.title: Configure Journal Entry Posting Approval from Store Template in Build Process Automation
entry.content:
In our case we were unable to utilize the Secure Network Connection (SNC) option, instead, we opted for a basic cloud connector connection to connect S/4 HANA on-premise system, which presented its own set of challenges. By referring to multiple external resources and blogs, we were able to successfully navigate through the configuration process and resolve the issues we encountered.

We hope that this blog post will serve as a valuable resource for anyone facing similar challenges in configuring journal entry posting from BPA Store. Let's get started

 

Step1

Prerequisites

The following SAP Business Technology Platform services are required to consume the content package Journal Entry Posting Approval:

 

  • SAP Build Process Automation to orchestrate the process.
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication
  • SAP S/4HANA, private edition
  • SAP Business Technology Platform, Cloud Foundry runtime
  • SAP Work Zone, standard or advanced edition for accessing My Inbox and Process Visibility workspaces.
  • SAP Business Application Studio for SAP UI5 application 
  • SAP Build Process Automation instance
  • SAP Document Management Service
  • SAP Desktop Agent 3.19 or higher – need to install in desktop or laptop.

 

Step 2

Destination Configuration

  1. Setup S/4 HANA - S4HANA_JOURNALENTRY_MG4

Pointing to S4 HANA on-premises system. And it should be configured in cloud connector. You have to use virtual host and port configured in cloud connector in URL.

 

Name

S4HANA_JOURNALENTRY_MG4

Type

HTTP

Proxy Type

On-Premise

User

<Onpremise-User>

Password

<Onpremise-Password>

Authentication

Basic Authentication

URL

<URL configured in Cloud connector for S4 HANA>

Additional Properties

 

HTML5.DynamicDestination: true,

sap-client:<S4 Client>,

sap-platform: abap,

sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled: true,

sap.processautomation.enabled: true,

WebIDEEnabled: true,

WebIDEUsage: odata_abap,dev_abap

Once created destination, check connection. You should get below success message

This template uses APIs and communication scenarios & these APIs are needed to be configured in SAP S/4HANA. The detail of these APIs  and required roles , cloud connector configuration you can refer configuration guide given in store from Page No 7 to 15.

  1. Document Management

The Input Template document for Sales Contract and any supporting documents can be uploaded in the approval form which are then made available as attachments for review and approval to the respective participants. For this you will need document management service and a corresponding destination in SAP BTP to connect to repository . But this step is optional.   

Create Instance for Document Management Service, under Instances and subscriptions. 

Go to service instances and search for Document Management Service, Integration Option. Click instances of DMS. You will see instance details as below

Under Service Keys, click three dots which will show popup with View option. Click View.

Copy highlighted values from service key as per below screenshot

Please follow below help link for further information

https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/create-repository-in-sap-document-management-service

Once subscribed to DMS, create destination named "sap_process_automation_document_store" and bind the respective values for client id, client secret & URL from document management service instance. 

Name

sap_process_automation_document_store

Type

HTTP

URL

Endpoints.ecmservice.url

Proxy Type

Internet

Authentication

Oauth2ClientCredentials

Client ID

uaa.clientid

Client Secret

uaa.clientsecret

Token Service URL

uaa.url + /oauth/token

3. SAP BPA Destination

sap_process_automation_service

If you are already having build process automation subscription, by default sap_process_automation_service destination will be available under destintation. Otherwise you can create by following below steps.

From BTP Cockpit select Instances and Subscription . Search for SAP Build Process Automation.

Select instance.

Click three dots in instance key -> Select View

Create destination by using service key details highlighted in screenshot. Respective path also given below.

 

Name

sap_process_automation_service

Type

HTTP

URL

Endpoint.api + /workflow/rest/v1

 

Proxy Type

Internet

Authentication

Oauth2ClientCredentials

ClientID

uaa.clientid

Client Secret

uaa.clientsecret

Token Service URL

 

Additional Properties

·         AppgyverEnabled: true

·         Endpoints: { "api":   <endpoints.api>}

·         html5-apps-repo:  {         "app_host_id": <html5-apps-repo.app_host_id>}

·         HTML5.DynamicDestination: true

·         Saasregistryenabled: true

·         sap.cloud.service: com.sap.spa.processautomation

·         sap.cloud.service.alias: spa

·         sap.processautomation.enabled: true

·         WebIDEEnabled: true

Remember while copying html5-apps-repo copy entire value starting from{.

Ex :  { “hostid” , “host id}

Similar way for endpoints copy entire value  {

Ex: {         "api":  <endpoint>     }

And then check connection. You will see below message.

4. SAP Build Process Automation Email

Configure email destination which will be used to send email to the approver, requestor, and admin at different stages of the process execution

Follow the steps to Configure a SMTP Mail Destination in your BTP account.

https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/configuring-smtp-mail-destination

Step 3

 

Configure APIs in SAP S/4HANA

This template uses the following APIs and communication scenarios & these APIs are needed to be configured in SAP S/4HANA. The detail of these APIs and required roles , cloud connector configuration you can refer configuration guide given in store from Page No 7 to 15

Step 4

Create Project from template in BPA

 

Goto Build Process Automation Cockpit, in left side pane, you can see Store

Click on Store - > and Enter Journal Entry Posting in search bar. Select first project in which publisher is SAP Build Process Automation.

You will navigate to this below page, where description of project, artifacts involved, configuration guide, required dependencies detailed out

You can download configuration guide by clicking pdf icon under Documents section

Now choose Create from Template option in top right corner of the page. You will get below Popup. Give your project name and click create.

Now from lobby you can see project got created. Click on project name.

You will be taken to overview page

Click icon nearby Overview. And you get to see artifacts overview in the below format.

Step 5

Deploy Project

Once imported, release and deploy from the Lobby. Before deployment, have to do some changes in determine approver step to change approver email id of workflow.

Configuration guide has brief steps from page 16 to 18. You can follow the same steps.

In journal Entry posting, action group created based on SAP SNC (Single Sign On) and SAP basic connection.

Either one of them required to run application. If you are using cloud connector configuration SNC is not required in this case.

Document Check – Using SAP basic connection

Document Check (SNC) – Using Secure Network Communication

Document Post – Using SAP basic connection

Document Post(SNC) - Using Secure Network Communication

In workflow, SNC based action group is used. Have to change it to basic connection based action group.

Replace existing SNC action group in (Journal Entry Document Check) automation with Document Check

For that, from artifacts select Journal Entry Document Check highlighted as below

In the below screen, remove action group BAPI DOCUMENT_CHECK (SNC). Drag and drop BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_CHECK under Document Check as below.

Fill in required parameters in right pane.

Parameters:

Connection Type

Basic

Message Server

<Backend System server IP>

Instance Number

<Backend System Instance No>

Client

<Backend System Client>

User

< Backend System login user>

Password

< Backend System login password>

Next step is bind the value for BAPIResponse. Once replaced SNC with Document Check, will get error in some of steps. Let’s resolve.  Click step Set Variable: BAPI Response. Select edit icon highlighted below in screenshot

Edit expression as per below screenshot

Similar way change set Variable document Error by following screenshots.

Go to Overview page of Journal Entry Posting and select Journal Entry Document Post automation as highlighted below.

Delete current action group in step 5 and drag and drop BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST in same step.

Select step 5. And fill details like message server (s4 hana system IP address), instance number, client, username and password

Once changed document post, will get some errors in other steps. Follow below steps to fix errors. Click step no 8 and in right pane, select highlighted icon to edit value

Change value as per below screenshot

Select step 9. And edit value from right pane.

Change expression as per below screenshot and save.

Journal Document Entry Post Automation:

Post completing all the changes, process needs to be released and deployed from SAP Build to reflect the changes.

While deploying you will get below popup, choose destination which is pointing to cloud connector URL of S4 HANA System.

Step 6

Deploy UI5 applications into BTP Cockpit

From artifacts, we have to deploy ui5 application into BTP cockpit from Business Application Studio.

Click Journal-Entry-Posting-JournalEntryApproval_ui.tar. Click Download button in below screen. Similarly download Journal-Entry-Posting-JournalEntryApproval_ui.tar as well.

Go to Business Application Studio. Extract downloaded mtar file. From BAS Click File-> Import Project -> select extracted project zip file.

Open mta.yaml file and change SBPA-instance to your build process automation instance name . You can find instance name under instances in BTP cockpit  as below.

 

SAP Build Process Automation as the successor product of SAP Workflow Management has Workflow, Business Rules and Process Visibility capabilities of Workflow Management

In this, we are going to use Build process automation capabilities not workflow management.So we have to replace bpmworkflowruntime destination with BPA destination.

Go to your xs-app.json.  change 1st route pointing to bpmworkflowruntime with below

{

       "source": "^/sap_process_automation_service/(.*)$",

        "target": "$1",

        "destination": "sap_process_automation_service",

        "authenticationType": "xsuaa",

        "csrfProtection": false

    },

Once  replaced,  your xs-app.json will look like below .

Destination S4HANA_JOURNALENTRY_MG4 which we have created earlier in cockpit for S4 HANA on-premise system.

 sap_process_automation_service is destination which will be created by default while you are running booster for build process automation.Go to CreateView.Controller.

Existing

In getWorkflowRuntimeBaseURL change url as below

URL in destination has path till v1<bpa endpoint>/workflow/rest/v1

So just replace /bpmworkflowruntime/public/workflow/rest/v1

Go to startWorkflowInstance function and replace definitionId with yours. You can get definition id from below path SAP BPA->Monitoring - >Processes and Workflows .

This definitionId will be available once you have deployed project .

Comment out header section. Token fetch call is configured in destination level. So this section not required in our case.

Once completed all the above changes deploy application into Cockpit.

 

Another mtar file named Journal-Entry-Posting-JournalEntryApproval_ui.tar. Extract it and import zip file into BAS. Then deploy. No changes required in approval level.  In case you need additional information add it and deploy.

 

 

 

Step 7

Agent Registration

Before running application, go to desktop agent installed in your local machine. Select Settings -> mode and activate Unattended mode. Because Document Check and Document Post are unattended automation which will be triggered in background once we submitted journal entry from UI5 application.

Next step have to add our agent to agent management. Go to build process automation lobby. Choose Control Tower then Agents under Agent Configuration.

Select Register Agent

After registration, you can see your laptop hostname under Items.

For further details on this , please follow this link https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_ONE/68a2e87fb29941b5bf959a184d9c6727/972ce532e66d48d8a7b43236fbb50248.html

Step 8

Testing Journal Entry Posting

Good to go. Now we can run application directly from deployed url in BTP -> HTML5 applications.

If you want to configure Journal Entry ui5 application in Build work zone standard edition, please follow the steps in this blog

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/integrate-with-work-zone..html

To enable My Inbox, Process Workspace, Monitor Workflows, follow the steps in this blog

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-configure-workzone.html

 

Go to BTP Cockpit. Choose HTML5 applications. Search for Journal

Click JournalEntryStartUimodule. Once application loaded, you could see below screen.

You have two options to post journal entry. Either manually enter data in form or upload excel. Excel template you can find in Project artifacts

Choose Upload in Posting Via drop down. Then upload file and submit.

Once submitted, from build process automation lobby, go to monitoring and select highlighted icon as shown in pic

My Inbox application will open in another tab.

In My Inbox, you can see new task. Enter required comments  and Approve the task.

Incase any issues, you didn’t get any task in My Inbox, from lobby go to monitoring -> Click Process and Workflow Instances.

In instances filter definition choose journal entry posting approval process , instance filter status select all . Tasks will be listed down. Choose task and see flow of execution and where it got failed

Step 9

Check journal entries from Fiori Launchpad

Open SAP Fiori launchpad from backend system. From launchpad select highlighted tile. And you can see journal entry posted from our end. Successfully we have configured Journal Entry .

Successfully configured journal entry posting from bot store.

entry.title: Product Updates for SAP Build Code – January 2025 Edition
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

Welcome to the first SAP Build Code release communications blog post of the new year! We're excited to kick off 2025 with many great new features and enhancements designed to streamline your development experience.

Dive into our new Project Overview providing a streamlined and comprehensive view on your projects. We have also made several improvements to simplify your work with Remote Function Calls (RFC). Another notable addition is the inline editing capability for CDS entities, enabling you to add or edit properties directly in the CDS Graphical Modeler. In addition to these exciting new features, we have also curated a collection of informative blog posts that delve into various aspects related to SAP Build Code.

To explore these features in depth and stay ahead of the curve, check out the details of what's new!

 

The new Project Overview

In our latest release of SAP Build Code, we've introduced an exciting change to the default left pane view. We've switched from the Project Explorer to the new Project Overview. This new Project Overview layout aligns more closely with the Storyboard aesthetics and not only includes all the current functionalities of the Project Explorer but also offers additional capabilities.

As you can see in the screenshot below, the Project Overview presents the following sections: Data Models, Services, Analytical Models, User Interface, and External Resources.

Sections of the Project Overview

You can perform project level actions from the context menu of the project, create new objects of each type from the section ‘+’ action, and perform additional actions from the context menu on each object.


Performing Actions in the Project Overview

Moreover, we are thrilled to announce that in the future the Project Overview will support not just projects created via the SAP Build Code Lobby or those using the "full-stack with productivity tools" template, but it will also support full-stack applications built on SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) Java or CAP Node.js. Stay tuned for these upcoming enhancements, and we hope you enjoy the improved interface!

 

Improvements when Working with RFC

When you are working with Remote Function Calls (RFCs) in your Full-Stack Application Using Productivity Tools projects you should try out the following enhancement: When adding a Remote Function Call to a Full-Stack Application Using Productivity Tools project, the run configuration for the external resource is now updated automatically. You can use the run configuration to select which destination to use for RFC when previewing the full-stack application.

For Remote Function Calls, both basic authentication and now also principal propagation are supported, allowing you to explore services in the Service Center, preview apps via run configuration, and run deployed apps seamlessly without the hassle of entering user credentials. For further information, see Explore SAP System Functions.


Run Configuration: Functions (RFC)

 

Inline Editing for CDS Entities

We are thrilled to announce the launch of our new inline editing feature in the CDS Graphical Modeler! This enhancement allows users to add or edit properties directly within an entity in the diagram shown in the CDS Graphical Modeler. This means fewer clicks, less navigating and a more intuitive experience. This addition complements the existing property sheet functionality and will make your work smoother and faster.


Adding a new property

 

Deprecation of Java Tools in SAP Web IDE

We'd like to inform you of important updates affecting SAP Web IDE: SAP Web IDE sunset will take place as part of the sunset of the SAP BTP, Neo environment by December 31, 2028. As a successor of SAP Web IDE, SAP introduced the SAP Business Application Studio five years ago, which has been serving as a robust and modernized alternative for development needs.

Furthermore, we'd like address the recent announcement regarding the deprecation of the SAP Java Buildpack 1. This component is set to be deprecated from the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment by June 2025. Consequently, Java tools currently supported in SAP Web IDE will also be phased out by the same date in June 2025.

For a more detailed examination of these changes, how it may affect your development process, and guidance on migrating to the recommended environments, please read Liat Borenstein’s comprehensive article: Deprecation of Java Tools in SAP Web IDE.

 

Further readings

  • Overview of GenAI in SAP Build and SAP Build Code 

Since its initial release in March 2024, SAP Build Code has rapidly evolved to bring cutting-edge generative AI (GenAI) functionalities to developers across diverse use cases. The latest blog post of my colleagues David Bizer and Marc Huber delves into the current features of SAP Build Code and the wide array of use cases they support. They also provide recommendations tailored to specific functionalities to ensure you get the most out of this powerful tool.

If you are interested in an overview on generative AI in SAP Build, you shouldn’t miss this blog post written by my colleague Marc Huber: AI in SAP Build - Overview and Best Practices.

  • Multi-Agent Support for Advanced CAP Application Generation

Streamline your development process by using the new feature for multi-agent support for advanced CAP application generation with SAP Build Code Joule. The key: harness specialized agents for various tasks using just a single prompt. In his latest blog post, Xin Ye explains this new feature in detail by guiding through an end-to-end flow.

  • Migrating SAPUI5 Applications to TypeScript with the Help of Joule 

As you probably know, Joule supports you with writing freestyle SAPUI5 code in SAP Build Code. Joule's latest feature offers an AI-driven migration of UI5 applications to TypeScript, which, compared to JavaScript, enables an improved development efficiency. This blog post, written by Martin Haeuser is dedicated to providing insights into the latest feature that offers AI-driven migration of SAPUI5 applications to TypeScript.

  • Differences between Extension Projects and Adaptation Projects in SAPUI5 

Looking to enhance your SAPUI5 applications? The blog post "Extending SAPUI5 apps: Adaptation projects vs. Extension projects" written by my colleague Oliver Graeff, explores the flexibility of SAPUI5, which allows not just developers but also key users and end users to extend standard apps. Discover how developers harness the power of adaptation projects in SAP Business Application Studio, the development environment of SAP Build Code. While adaptation projects offer advanced, strategic solutions for code-based extensions, there are also the predecessor extension projects. To address frequent confusions, Oliver explains the differences between the two of them.

  • Deprecation of the karma-ui5 Plugin 

SAPUI5 provides many testing options, like unit and integration tests, which are summarized in UI5 Testing. As the Karma test runner is deprecated since April 2023, the UI5 team now also has to deprecate karma-ui5. For further details, please read the following blog post from Oliver Graeff:  Deprecation of karma-ui5 plugin.

  • SAP Fiori tools 2501 Update 

SAP Fiori tools is consistently evolving, bringing innovative features that empower both experienced developers and casual users. The recent updates focus on harnessing AI, simplifying the user interface for adaptations, and enhancing the overall usability of the tools. Read this blog post written by Christoph Gollmick and discover how these new enhancements can elevate your SAP Fiori development journey.

  • SAP HANA SQL Notebooks in SAP Build, SAP Business Application Studio and Visual Studio Code

With SQL Notebooks you can incorporate SQL statements into your projects. It offers effortless access to your underlying SAP HANA database or HDI Container but also includes scripts for setup, adding test data, and delivering educational functions. In this blog post, Volker Saggau provides a comprehensive overview of SAP HANA SQL Notebooks in various environments such as SAP Build, SAP Business Application Studio, and Visual Studio Code.

 

For further information on new features, please check our roadmaps: roadmap for SAP Build Code, SAPUI5 Road Map. And have a look at What's New in SAP Build Code, the What's New Viewer for SAPUI5 and the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model Release Notes. If you want to stay up-to-date with news, learning resources, and product and strategy updates for SAP Build Code: follow our tag.

We look forward to seeing how you use these new capabilities to create innovative solutions and drive your projects forward. Stay tuned for more updates and happy coding! 😊

entry.title: Common Configuration Issues When Using Git in SAP Business Application Studio
entry.content:
Introduction

Although Git may seem like a trivial feature in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS), it plays a critical role in project development. It is an essential tool for collaborating with your development team. A typical developer’s day often starts with a git pull to stay up to date and ends with a git push to share their progress.

However, encountering unexpected errors during these operations can be frustrating. Interestingly, Git-related issues generate a disproportionately high number of customer support tickets in BAS compared to other components.

According to the SAP BAS document on Connecting to a Corporate Git Repository, when using an on-premise Git backend, Cloud Connector (SCC) and a properly configured subaccount destination are required to allow BAS, a cloud application, to access it. In this article, I will highlight some common Git connection issues caused by incorrect destination and SCC configurations and how to resolve them.

1. Incorrect Destination Properties

When configuring the destination, ensure that the following properties are correctly set:

  • WebIDEEnabled = true
  • HTML5.DynamicDestination = true

Failing to configure these properties correctly can cause BAS to fail when connecting to your on-premise Git system, often resulting in 500 or 502 errors depending on whether http or https is used in the Git command.

  • If your destination URL uses HTTP (http://), you may encounter: 

 

 

 

  • If your destination URL uses HTTPS (https://), you might see: 

Note The ProxyType of an On-Premise destination is HTTP, but the communication between BAS cloud application and SCC is encrypted and safe, there's no much difference whether the destination URL and the Git command uses "http://" or "https://". 

Besides above two properties, misuse of other properties can cause failures too. In one support case, a customer said they correctly set up Cloud Connector and the subaccount destination but still encountered a 500 Internal Server Error. Even with detailed logging enabled, git clone provided no additional clues beyond the error message. Git requests never reached the Cloud Connector at all. After extensive troubleshooting together with various teams, we discovered that the customer had mistakenly set the HTML5.ForwardAuthToken property to true. This property is designed to send oAuth token to the destination, it should not be turned on for an on-premise type destination or a destination with NoAuthentication because there's no oAuth token to forward at all.

The customer wasn’t sure why or when they added this property, but simply removing it resolved the issue.

2. Letter case mismatch in hostname

To expose an on-premise Git system, you must map it to a virtual host (hostname + port) in SCC. This virtual host is used when defining the destination URL and in Git commands.

While hostnames are generally case-insensitive, different behaviors across SCC, BTP Cockpit, and BAS can cause unexpected errors.

SCC: Uppercase letters are not allowed in both virtual and internal hostnames, the Administration Console converts them to lowercase.

BTP Cockpit: Allows uppercase letters in the hostname for the destination URL, the check connection test may still succeed if there're uppercase letters in the URL.

SAP BAS: Treats hostnames in the destination URL as case-insensitive.

For example, if your destination URL is "https://mygit:443", BAS will successfully match it, regardless of how you type it in Git commands:

  • git clone https://MyGit:443 
  • git clone https://myGit:443 
  • git clone https://MYGIT:443 

However, SCC (Expose Intranet Systems) requires that the destination URL hostname exactly matches the virtual hostname defined in Cloud Connector. A mismatch can result in 500 or 502 errors.

Recommendation  

To avoid inconsistencies, always use lowercase hostnames when configuring SCC mappings, destination URLs, and Git commands in BAS. This reduces the risk of unexpected errors caused by inconsistent case handling across different systems.

3. Duplicate destinations with the same hostname

When executing a Git command, BAS generally selects a destination whose URL matches both the hostname and port number in the Git command. If there's no port number specified, it defaults to 443 for https and 80 for http.

In BTP, you can create multiple destinations with the same hostname but different port numbers or properties. However, this can lead to unexpected behavior when using Git in BAS. It may unpredictably routing your Git command through an unintended destination to a wrong backend system.

Example Scenario

Destinations Defined in BTP

NameURL
Dest1http://git.int.mycompany.com:8088
Dest2http://git.int.mycompany.com:44300
Dest3http://git.int.mycompany.com:443

Resource mappings in SCC

 Virtual HostInternal HostProtocol
Resource1git.int.mycompany.com:8088localhost:3001https
Resource2git.int.mycompany.com:44300localhost:3002https
Resource3git.int.mycompany.com:443localhost:443https

When running Git commands in my BAS: 

BAS always used the third destination (Dest3), routing requests to localhost:443, regardless of the specified different port. However, after deleting Dest3, the first two Git commands correctly reached their intended destinations (localhost:3001 and localhost:3002). A little wierd!😵? 

Recommendation

To avoid confusion caused by above behavior:

  • Create only one destination per virtual host.
  • Avoid relying on port numbers for differentiation (even though SCC supports it).
  • When adding a new resource mapping in SCC, use a new virtual hostname instead of different ports.

4. Host In Request Header in SCC

In SCC, there's a hostInHeader property in resource mapping, corresponding to the "Host In Request Header" field in SCC Admin Console.

This property controls the value of the Host http request header before SCC forwardsthe request to the backend.  If hostInHeader is virtual, the Host header will be the virtual host (same as what you use in BAS git commands, no modification); otherwise SCC modifies the Host header to the internal host.

Although this setting typically doesn’t affect most web servers, some systems impose strict security policies requiring that the Host header matches their website hostname. In such cases, setting hostInHeader = internal is necessary to prevent 503 Service Unavailable or other errors.

Conclusion

By following recommendations in this article, understanding and properly configuring destination and SCC, removing duplicate destinations, you can avoid common Git-related issues and have a pleasant experience working with Git in SAP BAS.

entry.title: Step-by-Step Migration: From SAP Web IDE (Neo) to BAS (CF)
entry.content:
With the end of SAP Web IDE (Neo) coming soon, it's crucial to move your development projects to SAP Business Application Studio (CF). This guide will help you migrate your Fiori project quickly and easily, ensuring you can continue developing without any hiccups.

Prerequisites:

Ensure that the required destination is created in Cloud Foundry within your BAS subaccount.

Step 1: Exporting and Extracting the Project

  • Export your project from SAP Web IDE.
  • Extract the downloaded project file.

 

 Step 2:Setting Up the Development Space

  • In SAP BAS, create a new development space or use an existing one.

 

Step 3:Importing the Project

  • Drag and drop the extracted project file into the project pane in SAP BAS.
  • A popup will automatically appear to open the migration tool.

Manually Opening the Migration Tool

  • If needed, press Ctrl + Shift + P and search for "Fiori migration tool" to open it manually.

 

Step 4:Starting the Migration

  • Select the appropriate system, destination, and SAPUI5 version.
  • Click on "Start Migration".

 

Step 5:Installing Dependencies

  • Ensure that all dependencies listed in the package.json file are installed.
  • If there are any errors in the package.json, install the missing dependencies to resolve them.

 

Step 6:Previewing & Deploying the Application

  • Run ‘npm run start’ in the terminal to preview the application.
  • To deploy the application, you must create a deployment configuration from the application information section.

Common Issues:

 Resolving Theme/CSS Issues

  • If there are theme/CSS issues, search for the theme (e.g., sap_fiori_3) in the migrated project and replace it with a compatible theme for your SAP S/4HANA system.
  • Refresh the preview page to see the changes.

Data Flow Issues:

  • If the metadata isn't being called correctly, Follow the below steps
  1. Open Application Info.
  2. Navigate to Manage Service Models.
  3. Select the appropriate destination.
  4. Click Refresh & Save.
  5. You must stop and restart the preview to see the service changes.

Inode Issues:

  • During the migration of multiple applications, you might encounter the error "no space left on device" even if there is sufficient disk space. This is usually due to running out of inodes, which are used to track files and directories on your disk.

    1. Check Inode Usage: Open the terminal in SAP BAS and run the command df -i to check inode usage.

    2. Identify Files Consuming Inodes: Use the command find /home/user/projects -type f -size -1M to list small files.

    3. Remove Unnecessary Files: Delete log files, temporary files, and other small files that are no longer needed.

    bash
    find /home/user/projects -type f -name "*.log" -mtime +30 -exec rm -f {} \;find /home/user/projects -type f -name "tmp_*" -deletefind /home/user/projects -type f -name "*.bak" -delete

           4.Optimize Projects: Archive old projects or consolidate small files into larger files to reduce inode usage.

  • bash
    tar -czvf archived_project.tar.gz /home/user/projects/your_projectrm -rf /home/user/projects/your_project

                 5.Monitor Inode Usage: Regularly monitor inode usage to prevent this issue from recurring.

Note: Ensure all dependencies are installed as per the package.json file. If there are any errors, install the missing dependencies to fix them.

entry.title: The Future of Business: Exploring SAP's Business Technology Platform
entry.content:
This blog serves as a foundational guide for all professionals seeking to understand the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). 

SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform)

  • SAP Business Technology Platform brings together intelligent enterprise application with database and data management, analytics, integration and extension capabilities into one platform for both cloud and hybrid environments, including hundreds of pre-built integrations for SAP and third-party applications
  • In sap, Discovery center is a place where you can check the all the services which sap is providing.

Included in Business Technology Platform

  • Analytics - augmented BI, Collaborative planning, Predictive analytics and data warehousing
  • Database and data management - data pipelining, data virtualization, data governance, storage and processing, connect management, data orchestration and metadata management
  • Intelligent technologies - data-driven insights, intelligent RPA, artificial intelligence and IoT cloud and edge
  • Application development and integration - integration suite, intelligent BPM, enterprise solutions and digital experience


Solutions and Services:

  • SAP BTP offers fast in-memory processing, sustainable, agile and solutions and services to integrate data and extend applications, and fully embedded analytics and intelligent technologies.
  • services enable, facilitate, or accelerate the development of business applications and other platform services on SAP BTP

Regions:

  • You can deploy applications in different regions. each region represents a geographical location (example: Europe, US East) where applications, data, or services are hosted.
  • Regions are provided either by SAP or by our Infrastructure-as-a-service (IaaS) partners Amazon Web Services (AWS), Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud, and Alibaba Cloud. The third-party region providers operate the infrastructure layer of the regions, whereas SAP operated the platform layer and cloud foundry.
  • A region is chosen at the subaccount level. For each subaccount, you select exactly one region (that is one data center).

    Region:

Environments:

  • Environments constitute the actual platform-as-a-service offering of SAP BTP that allows for the development and administration of business applications. environments are anchored in SAP BTP on subaccount level.

  • Cloud foundry Environment: The cloud foundry environment enables you to develop new business applications and business services, supporting multiple runtimes, programming languages, libraries, and services
  • ABAP Environment: It allows you to create extension for ABAP-based products, such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and develop new cloud applications. You can transform existing ABAP-based custom code or extension to the cloud.
  • Kyma Environment: The Kyma environment is a fully managed Kubernetes runtimes based on the open-source project "Kyma" that allows developers to extend SAP solutions with serverless functions and combine them with containerized microservices
  • Neo Environment: The Neo environment let you develop HTML5 and SAP Hana extended application services (SAP HANA XS) applications. You can also use the UI Development Toolkit for HTML5(SAPUI5) to develop rich user interfaces for modern web0based business applications

Enterprise and Trail Accounts:

Trail Accounts

Enterprise Account

Trail account lets you try out the platform for free

An enterprise account is usually associated with one SAP customer or partner

Access is open to everyone

It contains their purchased entitlements

Trail accounts are intended for personal exploration, and not for production use or team development

It groups together different subaccounts that an administrator makes available to users for deploying applications

Commercial models:

Global Enterprise Account:

  • Consumption based - Pay-as-you-go
  • subscription based – subscription

Account Model Feature Set A:

  • Global Account is the realization of a contract you made with SAP. A global account is used to manage subaccounts, members, entitlements and quotas. You receive entitlements and quotas to use platform resources per global account and then distribute the entitlements and quotas to the subaccount for actual consumption.
  • Subaccounts let you structure a global account according to your organization's and project's requirements with regard to members, authorizations and entitlements.

Account Model Feature Set B:

 

Entitlements and Quotas:

  • When you purchase an enterprise account, you're entitled to use a specific set of resources, such as the amount of memory that can be allocated to your applications.
  • An entitlement is your right to provision and consume a resource, in other words, entitlements are the service plans that you're entitle to use.
  • A quota represents the numeric quantity that defines the maximum allowed consumption of a resource. In other words, how much of a service plan you're entitled to use.

User and Member Management:

  • Member management happens at all levels from global account to space, while user management is done for deployed applications.
  • User accounts enable users to log on to SAP BTP and access subaccounts and use services according to the permissions given to them.

  • Platform Users are usually developers, administrators or operators who deploy, administer, and trouble shoot applications and services on SAP BTP
  • Business Users use the applications that are deployed to SAP BTP. For example, the end users of your deployed applications or users of subscribed apps or services, such as SAP Business Application Studio or SAP Web IDE, are business users.
  • User Management refers to managing authentication and authorization for your business users. This is only done for your deployed applications.

Tools, Programming Models, Programming Languages, and APIs:

  • Tools - SAP BTP includes many tools to help you develop and manage applications and connect them to your on-premise systems
  • Programming Languages - SAP BTP supports many different programming languages; the availability of each depends on the development environment you're using.
  • Cloud Application Programming: The cloud Application Programming offers a consistent end-to-end programming model that includes languages, libraries, and APIs that are tailored for full-stack development on SAP BTP.
  • Continuous Integration and Delivery (CI/CD) - Depending on your use case, you can choose between different offerings for continuous integration and delivery.
  • APIs - Discover and consume APIs to manage, build, and extend the core capabilities of SAP BTP.
  • Cloud Management Tools- Feature Set Overview - Cloud management tools represent the group of technologies designed for managing SAP BTP. (IPS/IAS: Identity provider management system/Identity Access management system).
  • Prerequisites and Restrictions - Find a list of the product prerequisites and restrictions for SAP BTP.

SAP BTP Account Structure:

  • The SAP BTP Global Account is the highest level of account management and can be of type 'enterprise' or 'trail'.
  • A trail account allows you to try out many of the SAP XaaS for free, whereas an enterprise account is associated with a customer or partner and is the level at which resource and services can be purchased:

SAP BTP Account Structure:

  • Regions: Points of presence for service provisioning, Architecture is tailored to meet data, technical, and government requirements.
  • Sub Accounts: Technically independent accounts, with no sharing of users, applications, authorizations, etc.

can be manged using directories and quotas. Useful for separating environments and logical work packages.

  • Space - Provides complete application isolation, allowing multiple instances to run independently.
  • Services - Applications can be created from service templates or custom applications can be deployed using containers.

Cloud Providers:

SAP BTP is a multi-cloud platform deployed on cloud providers;

  • SAP
  • Azure
  • Amazon Web Services
  • Google Cloud Platform (GCP)
  • Alibaba Cloud

The deployment of XaaS along with data consideration are just two factors to be addressed architecturally ensuring the requirements of government, business(functional) and technology(non-functional) are satisfied.

On-Premise to Public Cloud connectivity:

  • SAP BTP Cloud services can be used independently or with on-premise services to share data.
  • Communication between public services and SAP S/4HANA systems needs to be secured, which can be done using a reverse invoke proxy like SAP Cloud Connector.
  • SAP Cloud Connector establishes an outbound encrypted connection from on-premise to cloud, allowing data to be shared securely

What Does SAP BTP DO??

SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) provides a technical foundation for businesses to quickly change and adapt by offering a portfolio of SAP products, services, and solutions grouped under four technology pillars.

Database and Data Management:

  • This pillar consists of everything you need to store, access, process, integrate, and interpret all relevant data in your landscape.
  • The different services and products categorized under this pillar let you manage and use data in a flexible and efficient manner at the speed your business demands.

Analytics:

  • Analytics products enable business to plan, forecast, and make data-driven decisions.
  • SAP Analytics Cloud integrates data from various sources using machine learning technologies and enables users to ask questions in natural language
  • The analytics pillar also includes products such as SAP Data Warehouse Cloud, SAP BW/4HANA, and SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning.

Application Development and Integration:

  • The Key features and Products that are part of this pillar is integration, Extensibility, Business Services and more.

Intelligent Technologies:

  • Products that are part of this pillar is AI, ML and IOT

Developers:

  • For SAP Developers, it provides a strong product portfolio and a central platform for accelerating development and integration

Partners:

  • SAP Partners can leverage the tools and services that help accelerate development and integration in a more efficient way, reducing time to value and reducing implementation costs.
  • They can build extensions to existing SAP solutions that can be made available to customers to buy in the SAP Store. The common architecture and open framework help to reduce time to market.  

Customers:

  • SAP Customers can start with implementing one product and the add more to the landscape    building on their core processes, leveraging their existing long-term investments, or plugging in new business and technology innovations.
  • They can rapidly build, integrate, and extend application or consume content made available by partners.

Advantages of SAP Business Technology Platform:

 

    

Note:

  1. Most of the times you will face a error while opening BAS
  2. That you are not authorized access denied.
  3. Go to security
  4. Go to users
  5. Navigate to your user
  6. Click on role collection
  7. Assign Role
  8. Now you can open the BAS

Menu will be different if we open subaccount.

SAP BAS (Business Application Studio):

  • When you are referring to Dev Space then you are referring to development
  • When you are referring to Cloud foundry then you are referring to runtime
  • both are independent to each other.

Note:

If it is trail account you are allowed a total of 2 dev spaces with only 1 running at a time

  • You can download, delete, and change extensions. Renaming the dev space is not possible.
  • SAP BAS is essential for developing extension applications on SAP Business Technology Platform.
  • BAS provides a common development environment that runs independently of developers technically system setup and is tailored for business application development in the SAP ecosystem.
  • BAS can be accessed through an internet browser and offers a desktop-like development experience similar to Microsoft Visual Studio Code.
  • BAS offers all the necessary tools for business application development, including a command line and editors optimized for different use cases
  • A subscription and the appropriate authorizations are required for developers or business users to access SAP Business Application Studio in a subaccount, and administrators can distribute role collections via the security capabilities of SAP BTP.

There are three predefined roles for BAS:

  • Developer - Develop in BAS
  • Extension deployer - Develop and deploy BAS extensions
  • Administrator - Administer BAS (for example, restart dev spaces...)

BAS is a multiple environment development tool where we are having multiple extensions

Example: For fiori we have multiple options like predefined extensions as shown in below

  • To create dev Space, click on Create Dev Space.

  • Enter Dev space name and select any application based on the business and click on create dev space button.
  • Now it will create a dev space.

  • When creating a new dev space, you can choose a preconfigured development environment, based on the type of application that you want to build
  • There are several dev spaces available.

Example:

  1. SAP Fiori
  2. Full Stack Cloud Application
  3. SAP HANA Native Application
  • Each dev space type comes with a set of SAP predefined extensions, that are required for the respective application scenario.
  • You can select additional extensions to enhance your space.
  • Due to its open source nature and close resemblance to Microsoft Visual Studio Code, SAP Business Application Studio also lets you integrate Visual Studio Code-compatible extensions from the Open VSX Registry.
  • It is possible to create multiple dev spaces per user, depending on the chosen application plan.

 The following plans are available for subscription:

  • Free - Restricted up to two dev spaces, only one space running and a size limit of 4 GB
  • Standard-edition

  • Only in project folder we need to create (its recommended).

By default, we will be having. vscode file which is having all the extensions that are installed.

There are two ways for navigation:

  • wizard way
  • command prompt

Example:

  • To create a directory

             mkdir test

  • To navigate into created directory

             cd test

  • We have option for split in terminal so that we can use multiple options at the same we can work on different directories at a time.
  • SAP Business Application studio provides everything that a professional developer needs. it is a full development environment. (It replaces SAP Web IDE.)

SAP Business Application Studio has a lot of capabilities, including:

  • Project Explorer
  • Code editor
  • Terminal
  • Debugger
  • Code management integrations
  • And many more.

 Benefits of SAP Business Application Studio at a glance:

  • Provides a managed, preconfigured, hosted environment, optimized for SAP application development.
  • Can be centrally administered with tools, repositories, systems access, and company policies.
